summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 24 warning, 15 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 extensions.mjs:162 — Index-based extension-to-result matching is unsound: `callHook` filters null/undefined returns before pushing to `results`, but `runExecuteRun` reconstructs the extension list via `.filter()` and matches by position. If extension A returns null and B returns a command, B's output is attributed to A's name. Fix: return `{ ext, result }` pairs from `callHook` or iterate extensions directly in `runExecuteRun`.
🟡 extensions.mjs:177 — `execSync` with 30s timeout blocks the event loop per command; acceptable for v1 single-extension use but won't scale for outer-loop runs with multiple extensions.
🟡 run.mjs:1219 — `ext-{name}-run.txt` artifacts written to disk but not registered in handshake; carried unresolved from 3 prior review rounds. Harness and dashboard cannot discover these files.
🟡 extensions.mjs:166 — `Array.isArray(result)` support lets extensions return multiple commands, but SPEC.md:114 explicitly declares this out of scope. Update spec or remove array unwrapping.

🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:162-163` — Extension-to-result index mapping bug. `callHook` filters out failed/null results, but `runExecuteRun` uses positional indexing against the unfiltered extension list. When any hook throws, subsequent results get attributed to the wrong extension name.

🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:177` — `execSync` cannot capture stderr when the command exits 0. Only stdout is returned on success; stderr is silently discarded. Spec requires unconditional capture. Fix: use `spawnSync`.

🟡 `test/extension-system.test.mjs:688` — False positive test. The "captures stderr" test asserts `content.includes("err-msg")` but matches against the echoed command string (`$ echo err-msg >&2; exit 0`), not actual captured stderr. This masks the bug above.

🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:166,202` — Array command support silently overwrites the same artifact file. Code accepts `Array.isArray(result)` but spec says multi-command is out of scope. Each command clobbers the previous artifact.

🟡 `extensions.mjs:162` — Index-mapping bug: when multiple extensions have `executeRun` and one hook fails/returns null, subsequent results get attributed to the wrong extension (wrong artifact filename, wrong error message)
🟡 `extensions.mjs:199` — Artifact written to disk but never registered in handshake as `cli-output` type per spec. Flagged in all 3 prior rounds, unresolved.
🟡 `run.mjs:1225` — Raw extension output embedded in retry prompt without escaping triple-backtick sequences (prompt injection vector)

The core user value is delivered: extensions can run commands, output is captured, and `required: true` correctly blocks the task. All 593 tests pass. The prior round-3 🔴 blocker (dead-code traversal guard) is resolved by the refactoring into a single `extensions.mjs`. Three warnings go to backlog — none block merge for v1 where single-extension usage dominates.

🟡 extensions.mjs:162 — Result-to-extension index mapping is wrong when an extension returns null/undefined; `callHook` filters these from results but `extWithHook` still includes them, causing artifacts and failure messages to attribute to the wrong extension — fix by returning `{ext, result}` pairs from `callHook` or tracking indices explicitly

🟡 extensions.mjs:166 — Array command return path is handled but untested; multiple commands from the same extension overwrite the same artifact file, silently losing output — add test and index artifact filenames
🟡 test/extension-system.test.mjs:561 — All `runExecuteRun` tests use exactly 1 extension; no multi-extension test exists to expose the index-mapping bug — add a 2+ extension test
🟡 run.mjs:1223 — The executeRunFailed -> retry path has no integration test; a call-site regression is undetectable — add integration test

🟡 extensions.mjs:199 — `ext-run-*.txt` artifacts written to disk but never registered in handshake; spec says "stored as a cli-output artifact" implying discoverability (carried from prior reviews, still unresolved)

🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:162` — Extension-result index misalignment: `callHook` filters out null/failed results but `runExecuteRun` matches results to extensions by index, causing wrong extension attribution when some hooks return null or fail
🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:177` — No `maxBuffer` on `execSync` (default ~1MB); `runGateInline` uses 50MB but this was omitted here; large command output causes silent `ENOBUFS` failure
🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:202` — Artifact filename collision: array of commands from one extension all write to the same filename, each overwriting the previous
🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:203` — No sanitization of `desc.command` echoed into artifact file (control characters, length)
🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:202` — Artifact files written to disk but NOT registered in `handshake.json` (carried from prior parallel review, still unresolved)

🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:159-164` — Index-based extension attribution bug: `callHook` filters out null/error results, but `runExecuteRun` matches results to extensions by positional index. If any extension's hook throws or returns null, artifact filenames and error messages attribute commands to the wrong extension. Fix: return `{ext, result}` tuples from `callHook`.
🟡 `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:162` — `registry.extensions.filter(...)` computed inside the loop on every iteration; the filter is invariant since `callHook` already completed. Hoist before the loop.
🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:371` — `if (registry)` dead guard in `runParallelReviews`; `loadExtensions()` always returns a truthy object. Carried from prior review, unresolved.
